mnemonics: FIND FAST: Frequency-sorted Indexes Navigate Documents For Accelerated Search Times.
POSTINGS POINTER: Postings Lists Point Out Every Relevant Term In Numerous Documents.
keyPoints: An inverted index maps terms (words) to the list of documents (postings list) that contain them.
It consists of a dictionary (vocabulary) and postings lists which contain document identifiers and often positional information.
The primary purpose is to enable fast full-text searches, which is the foundation of modern search engines.
commonMistakes: Confusing the dictionary (list of terms) with the postings (list of documents for each term).
Forgetting that the index must be updated (a costly operation) when new documents are added to the collection.
